description_long: This machine gun features a robust, dual-barrel design with prominent ammunition belts feeding into each side, suggesting a high rate of fire. The main body is constructed from dark, matte-finished metal with visible wear, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. Large, boxy ammunition containers are mounted on either side, connected to the barrels by articulated feed mechanisms. The gun is supported by a sturdy, angular frame with two forward-extended legs, likely for stability during operation. A rectangular handle or support bar runs along the top, providing a grip or mounting point. The overall design is functional and militaristic, with exposed mechanical components and a focus on durability.
description: A heavy, dual-barrel machine gun with large side-mounted ammo boxes, articulated feed belts, and a rugged metal frame. The design is utilitarian, with exposed mechanisms and a matte, worn finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the twin barrels, symmetrical ammo boxes, and the articulated feed belts curving outward from the center.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the length of the barrels, the full arc of the ammo belt, and the boxy ammo container.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the ammo boxes, the frame structure, and the feed mechanisms leading into the gun.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the dual barrels, ammo belt, and the right-side ammo box in profile.
attribute_barrel_type: dual standard cylindrical barrels with exposed cooling fins
